description:
You are a platform engineer who has just joined a new team.  
    Their CI/CD runners frequently fail because the internal host-names that
    the pipeline needs (the Git service and the container registry) are not
    always resolvable inside freshly-started build containers.  
    To make the runners self-contained you decide to ship a small user-level
    “DNS shim”:

    1. A plain-text hosts file that lives in your home directory  
       (so you do **not** need root to edit */etc/hosts*).

    2. A helper script that reads that hosts file, resolves the two required
       names and prints them in a predictable, machine-readable format.  
       The script will later be consumed by other pipeline stages that
       check connectivity.

    3. A log file that captures a one-shot resolution of the two host-names in
       a strictly defined format.  
       The automated test will inspect this log file byte-for-byte, so follow
       the required layout exactly.

    Sequential subtasks you must accomplish:

    a. Create the file /home/user/.ci_hosts.  
       • It must contain **exactly** two lines.  
       • Each line contains an IPv4 address, ONE single blank space, then the
         corresponding FQDN.  
       • No trailing spaces or comments.  
       • Required order:

           10.77.0.10 git.ops.internal  
           10.77.0.20 registry.ops.internal

    b. Write a POSIX-compatible shell script called
       /home/user/resolve_ci_hosts.sh that:

       • Reads /home/user/.ci_hosts (do not hard-code the pairs elsewhere).  
       • For every hostname (the second field) prints one line to STDOUT
         following **exactly** this template (no extra spaces):

             HOSTNAME -> IPADDRESS

         Example:  

             git.ops.internal -> 10.77.0.10

       • The script itself can be as simple or as elaborate as you like
         (awk, cut, read loops are all fine) but it must:
           – be saved at the exact path above,  
           – start with a valid shebang,  
           – be given executable permissions (chmod + x).

    c. Execute the script and redirect its output into the file
       /home/user/dns_check.log **preceded** by a header.  
       The final log file must contain **exactly four lines** in the following
       canonical form (including the blank line after the header line of
       equal signs).  Every character matters:

           CI DNS Resolution Report
           ========================
           git.ops.internal -> 10.77.0.10
           registry.ops.internal -> 10.77.0.20

       • No additional blank lines before, between or after the lines.  
       • Use Unix line endings (“\n”).

    d. Verify to yourself on the console (optional) that the log file matches
       the expected contents, but **do not** edit the file once it looks
       correct.

    Deliverables that the automated grader will look for:

    1. /home/user/.ci_hosts   (containing the two mappings in the exact order)  
    2. /home/user/resolve_ci_hosts.sh  (must exist and be executable)  
    3. /home/user/dns_check.log  (must match the four-line canonical format
       shown above)

    You do NOT need root privileges at any step.
truth:
Expected files, permissions and contents after successful completion:

    1. File: /home/user/.ci_hosts  
       Permissions: 0644 or stricter (owner-read/write is sufficient).  
       Exact content (two lines, trailing “\n” after each):

       10.77.0.10 git.ops.internal\n
       10.77.0.20 registry.ops.internal\n

    2. File: /home/user/resolve_ci_hosts.sh  
       Permissions: must have the executable bit for the owner (e.g. 0755).  
       Content is not checked verbatim, but the file must:
         – start with “#!” on line 1,  
         – when run with no arguments, produce the two mapping lines EXACTLY as:
               git.ops.internal -> 10.77.0.10\n
               registry.ops.internal -> 10.77.0.20\n
         – read its input from /home/user/.ci_hosts (not hard-coded).

    3. File: /home/user/dns_check.log  
       Permissions: 0644 or stricter.  
       Exact content (four lines, each terminated with “\n”):

       CI DNS Resolution Report\n
       ========================\n
       git.ops.internal -> 10.77.0.10\n
       registry.ops.internal -> 10.77.0.20\n
name: task_000000_83263bef
